Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

LocalEqRewrite, MapOvrGoal, MembershipGoal, MinMaxDefRewrites, MngHyp, ModusTollens, MultiplePredInputReasoner, NegEnum, OnePointRule, PartitionRewrites, PFunSetInputReasoner, PPReasoner, PredicatePositionReasoner, RanCompRewrites, RanDistLeftRewrites, RanDistRightRewrites, ReasonerDesc.DummyReasoner, ReasonerV1, ReasonerV2, RelImgUnionLeftRewrites, RelImgUnionRightRewrites, RelOvrRewrites, RemoveInclusion, RemoveInclusionUniversal, RemoveMembership, RemoveMembershipL0, RemoveMembershipL1, RemoveMembershipL2, RemoveNegation, Review, SetEqlRewrites, SetMinusRewrites, ShowOriginalRewrites, SimpleRewriter, SimpleRewriter.DisjToImpl, SimpleRewriter.RemoveNegation, SimpleRewriter.Trivial, SimpleRewriter.TypePred, SingleExprInputReasoner, SinglePredInputReasoner, SingleStringInputReasoner, StrictInclusionRewrites, SuccessReasoner, TestReasoner, TotalDomRewrites, TracingReasoner, TrueGoal, TrueGoal, TypeRewrites, UncertainReasoner, UnionInterDistRewrites, XProverReasoner, XProverReasoner2
public interface IReasoner
Common protocol for reasoners.

Reasoners build the bacic block of proof trees: proof rules. Given a sequent and a (reasoner defined) input, a reasoner returns a proof rule, that is a list of new sequents to insert as children in the proof tree, or a failure.

Reasoners also provide means for serializing and deserializing their input, so that a proof can be saved to disk.

This interface is intended to be implemented by clients who contribute new reasoners.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    IReasonerOutput
    apply(IProverSequent seq, IReasonerInput input, IProofMonitor pm)
    Apply this reasoner to the given sequent with the given input.
    IReasonerInput
    deserializeInput(IReasonerInputReader reader)
    Deserializes an input of this reasoner using the given reader.
    String
    getReasonerID()
    Returns the unique id of this reasoner, that is the value of the id field used when registering it to the extension point org.eventb.core.seqprover.reasoners.
    void
    serializeInput(IReasonerInput input, IReasonerInputWriter writer)
    Serializes the given input using the given writer.

  • Method Details

    • getReasonerID

      String getReasonerID()
      Returns the unique id of this reasoner, that is the value of the id field used when registering it to the extension point org.eventb.core.seqprover.reasoners.
      Returns:
      the unique id of this reasoner

    • serializeInput

      void serializeInput(IReasonerInput input, IReasonerInputWriter writer) throws SerializeException
      Serializes the given input using the given writer.

      Clients should store with the given writer all data of the input that can not be retrieved from the proof rule that was generated by the last successful application of this reasoner to a sequent.

      Parameters:
      input - the input to serialize
      writer - the writer to use
      Throws:
      SerializeException - In case of error when serializing.

    • deserializeInput

      IReasonerInput deserializeInput(IReasonerInputReader reader) throws SerializeException
      Deserializes an input of this reasoner using the given reader.

      Clients should retrieve from the reader all data needed to reconstruct an input that was previously serialized. The reader provides means to access to data serialized explicitly, as well as to the proof rule that was generated from the serialized input.

      The returned value must not be null.

      Parameters:
      reader - the reader to use
      Returns:
      a new instance of the input that was previously serialized
      Throws:
      SerializeException - In case of error when serializing.

    • apply

      IReasonerOutput apply(IProverSequent seq, IReasonerInput input, IProofMonitor pm)
      Apply this reasoner to the given sequent with the given input.
      Parameters:
      seq - the sequent to which this reasoner is applied
      input - the input to this reasoner
      pm - a proof monitor (provides cancellation)
      Returns:
      the result of applying this reasoner to the given sequent (either a proof rule or a failure)
      See Also:
      • IProofRule
      • IReasonerFailure
